title: Deinandra greeneana
taxonomy: Deinandra greeneana is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Deinandra greeneana typically includes it as a member of the genus Deinandra, which is part of the tribe Madieae. This classification places it within the larger plant kingdom, specifically in the kingdom Plantae and the division Magnoliophyta. Additionally, it is classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage.

intro: Deinandra greeneana, also known as Greene's tarweed, is a flowering plant species native to California in the United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the relief of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Deinandra greeneana include sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Deinandra greeneana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
